title: On Wittgenstein's Last Notes (1950–51) On Certainty
Ethical Intuitionism II
abstract: South . So we have agreed to bury intuitionism. Well, I dare say it is right. But we ought to bury some of the grave-diggers too. Some of the things that Ross said are no doubt wrong, or at least misleading: but they are a lot less wrong than most of the things said since the war.
